---
title: "TensorColumnAnalogStorage"
---

## Overview

`TensorColumnAnalogStorage` is a zero-copy CRTP storage backend for
`AnalogTimeSeries` that directly references a column of a `TensorData` object.
It is part of Phase 3 of the
[multi-channel storage roadmap](../multi_channel_storage_and_transforms.qmd#sec-phase3).

**File:** `src/DataObjects/AnalogTimeSeries/storage/TensorColumnAnalogStorage.hpp`

## Purpose

When multi-channel data is stored as a `TensorData` (an Armadillo `arma::fmat`
with shape `[time × channels]`), individual channels need to be exposed as
`AnalogTimeSeries` objects for compatibility with `DataViewer_Widget`, transforms,
and export. `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` enables this without copying data.

## Memory Model

Armadillo uses column-major layout: each column of an `arma::fmat` is contiguous
in memory. `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` caches the raw column pointer
(`arma::fmat::colptr(c)`) to provide $O(1)$ element access and contiguous span
access.

```
arma::fmat(rows=1000, cols=32)  ← column-major

   col 0       col 1       col 2      ...
┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐
│ t0      │ │ t0      │ │ t0      │
│ t1      │ │ t1      │ │ t1      │
│ ...     │ │ ...     │ │ ...     │
│ t999    │ │ t999    │ │ t999    │
└─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘
     ↑            ↑
  _col_ptr     _col_ptr
  (col=0)      (col=1)
```

Each `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` holds a `std::shared_ptr<TensorData const>`,
ensuring the underlying matrix stays alive as long as any view exists.

## CRTP Interface

| Method | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `getValueAtImpl(i)` | `_col_ptr[i]` (Armadillo) or `TensorData::at({i, col})` (fallback) |
| `sizeImpl()` | `_tensor->numRows()` |
| `getSpanImpl()` | `{_col_ptr, numRows()}` if contiguous; empty otherwise |
| `getSpanRangeImpl(s, e)` | `{_col_ptr + s, e - s}` if contiguous; empty otherwise |
| `isContiguousImpl()` | `true` if Armadillo-backed |
| `getStorageTypeImpl()` | `AnalogStorageType::Custom` |
| `tryGetCacheImpl()` | Returns `{_col_ptr, numRows(), true}` if Armadillo |

: CRTP method implementations {.striped}

## Construction

```cpp
auto tensor_ptr = std::make_shared<TensorData>(some_tensor);
auto storage = TensorColumnAnalogStorage(tensor_ptr, /*column_index=*/0);
```

**Preconditions:**

- `tensor != nullptr`
- `tensor->ndim() == 2`
- `column_index < tensor->numColumns()`

## Factory Method

`TensorColumnAnalogStorage` is typically used through the general-purpose
`AnalogTimeSeries::createFromStorage` factory:

```cpp
auto storage = TensorColumnAnalogStorage(tensor_ptr, col);
auto wrapper = AnalogDataStorageWrapper(std::move(storage));
auto analog = AnalogTimeSeries::createFromStorage(std::move(wrapper), time_storage);
```

This factory was designed to avoid adding a TensorData dependency to the
AnalogTimeSeries library. The caller constructs the concrete storage and
wraps it in the type-erased `AnalogDataStorageWrapper` before passing it in.

## Integration: TensorToAnalog Transform

The `TensorToAnalog` transform (Phase 1, updated in Phase 3) uses this backend:

1. Copies the input `TensorData` into a `shared_ptr` (self-contained ownership).
2. Resolves time storage once (shared across all column views).
3. For each selected column, constructs a `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` and wraps
   it in an `AnalogTimeSeries` via `createFromStorage`.

The resulting `AnalogTimeSeries` objects share the same `TensorData` memory.

## Non-Armadillo Fallback

If the `TensorData` uses a storage backend other than `ArmadilloTensorStorage`
(e.g. `LibTorchTensorStorage`), the column pointer cache (`_col_ptr`) is `nullptr`.
In this case:

- `isContiguous()` returns `false`
- `getSpan()` / `getSpanRange()` return empty spans
- `getValueAt(i)` falls back to `TensorData::at({i, col})` (per-element virtual dispatch)

This ensures correctness for all backends while preserving zero-copy performance
for the common Armadillo case.

## Tests

17 Catch2 test cases in
`src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorToAnalog/TensorColumnAnalogStorage.test.cpp`
cover:

- Basic element access and size
- Zero-copy memory sharing (pointer comparison with `arma::fmat::colptr`)
- Contiguous span access (full and range)
- Edge cases (single column, single row, span boundaries)
- `createFromStorage` factory integration
- `TensorToAnalog` round-trip with zero-copy verification
- TimeFrame preservation
